# Continuity Audit AU-0001 — Animalia

**Date**: 2026-04-19
**Trigger**: periodic
**Parameters**: `briefs/animalia-audit-1-parameters.md` (audit_scope: all; severity_floor: 3; recent_canon_addition_cutoff: CH-0010)
**Findings**: 3 (sev-3: 2; sev-2: 1)
**Retcon cards emitted**: 1 (RP-0001)

---

## Parameters Interpretation Note

`recent_canon_addition_cutoff: CH-0010` places the cutoff at the most recent ledger entry. Under a strict "newer than cutoff" reading, the Phase 2 delta window would be empty. Given `audit_scope: all` and `trigger_context: periodic`, this audit interpreted the cutoff as an **inclusive anchor** — "audit the world state as of CH-0010" — and Phase 2 analyzed all 10 CH entries (CH-0001 through CH-0010). This interpretation is recorded so that future audits can parameterize cutoffs differently if desired (e.g., `recent_canon_addition_cutoff: CH-0007` would then mean "analyze CH-0008 through CH-0010" under strict reading).

---

The 10 lint questions produced candidate flags as follows. Each candidate was carried forward into its Phase 4 sub-category for confirmation or dismissal.

| # | Lint Question | Candidates Generated | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | What changed recently that should now appear elsewhere? | CH-0010's CF-0036 EVERYDAY_LIFE patches — 5 clusters addressed per adjudication PA-0009; verified via section-header grep | Cleared (confirmed per-cluster annotations present) |
| 2 | What capability has become suspiciously consequence-free? | CF-0021 crafter stream; CF-0028 expeditions; CF-0033 dragoons; CF-0034 banditry; CF-0035 mutated beasts; CF-0036 friction | All 6 feed Phase 6 Burden Debt scan |
| 3 | What institution has failed to respond to a world-changing development? | CF-0036 interspecies friction — institutional responses? | Cleared — INSTITUTIONS.md §Law/Custom line 45 + §Religion line 84 carry CF-0036 institutional response patches |
| 4 | Which facts are now redundant? | CH-0010 `affected_cf_ids` + `affected_fact_ids` redundancy | → F-01 |
| 5 | Which facts silently imply broader adoption? | CF-0033 dragoon adoption asymmetry; CF-0031 registry; CF-0036 supremacist doctrine fringe | Cleared — scope-qualified in domain files |
| 6 | Which regions are under-updated after a global change? | CF-0036 — all 5 clusters | Cleared via EVERYDAY_LIFE section headers |
| 7 | Which species or classes are absent from consequences they should feel? | Carnivore-folk vs CF-0035 trophy-display + CF-0036 cannibal-edge sub-subset | Cleared — PEOPLES line 137 carnivore-folk progenitor-cult taboo + line 138 CF-0036 firewall extension |
| 8 | Which earlier facts now need scoping, limiting, or reclassification? | CF-0031 / CF-0032 axis-count prose at CH-0005/CH-0006 | → F-02 |
| 9 | Which diegetic texts should now be re-read as biased or incomplete? | Vespera dossier MR-firewall scope limited to M-1…M-7 | → F-03 |
| 10 | Which mysteries should be protected from accidental overexposure? | M-1, M-2, M-5 (forbidden), M-10 (selection-bias firewall load-bearing since CH-0008), M-15 (rumor-only gargantuan-underground) | Cleared — all 18 MR entries carry disallowed-cheap-answer lists; CH-0008/CH-0009/CH-0010 firewall extensions add discipline rather than erode it |

## Per-Category Findings (Phase 4 sub-passes 4a–4j)

### 4a Ontological Contradictions — no findings

Scanned: all CFs with `type` in {species, entity, metaphysical_rule, hidden_truth} against INVARIANTS.md ONT-1/ONT-2/ONT-3. ONT-1 has four clarifications (CH-0002, CH-0004, CH-0009, implied fifth via CH-0010 SOC-1 "category error" framing) each of which preserves ONT-1 without exception. ONT-2 carries CH-0002 annotation. ONT-3 CH-0009 clarification preserves species reproductive boundaries. No contradictions.

### 4b Causal Contradictions — no findings

Scanned: all CFs with populated `costs_and_limits` against CAU-1/CAU-2/CAU-3. CAU-1 CH-0009 extension (cost-taxonomy: rare survival-as-mutation) preserves universality. CAU-3 ward-restriction discipline preserved across all diegetic artifacts and character dossiers (Vespera's "respects ward-specifics restriction" explicitly; Melissa's ARR "no ward markings at the lintel" at public-knowledge register only). No violations.

### 4c Distribution Contradictions — no findings

Scanned: all CFs with non-`global` geographic scope + non-`public` social scope against domain-file references. CF-0032 Rule-4 partial concession (sizing-drift to non-ancestral estates) is explicitly named as intended generational drift, not silent globalization. DIS-3 mythic-species firewall holds. SOC-4 artifact monopoly preserved via CF-0030/CF-0033 polity-asymmetric framing. No violations.

### 4d Timeline Contradictions — no findings

Scanned: CFs with temporal markers against TIMELINE.md four-layer structure. CF-0031 Charter-Era institutional residue annotation (TIMELINE line 47) is consistent. CF-0033 late-Incident-Wave founding annotation is coherent. CF-0034 Charter-Era bandit-corridor stabilization + Layer-3/Layer-4 recruitment intensification is coherent. No age-impossibility, no diffusion conflict.

### 4e Institutional Contradictions — no findings

Scanned: INSTITUTIONS.md institution entries against CFs citing institutional domains. Every CH-0004 through CH-0010 addition has a corresponding institutional response patch in INSTITUTIONS.md §Law/Custom, §Religion, §Military, or §Trade/Guilds. Rule 5 (No Consequence Evasion) satisfied for all six capability-stratum CFs (CF-0021, CF-0024, CF-0028, CF-0031, CF-0033, CF-0034, CF-0035, CF-0036).

### 4f Everyday-Life Contradictions — no findings

Scanned: EVERYDAY_LIFE.md per-cluster entries (5 clusters) against CFs whose `visible_consequences` would reshape ordinary life. CH-0004 through CH-0010 each carry per-cluster patches per adjudication records. Rule 2 (No Pure Cosmetics) satisfied — CF-0021 kiln-explosion injury register, CF-0024 contractor-empirical triage, CF-0031 ledger-mark civic texture, CF-0033 dragoon drill-bell + lintel-marker + skip-rope rhyme, CF-0034 bounty-wall + winter-emptied farmsteads, CF-0035 bounty-wall fourth column, CF-0036 tavern slur-threshold + crew-catechism + aftermath-household-vocabulary all change labor, clothing, or daily routine at cluster scale. *(Note: audit sampled EVERYDAY_LIFE via section headers + targeted reads on cluster boundaries and CF annotation presence; full cluster-text read was not performed given 116KB size. A deeper audit cycle on EVERYDAY_LIFE is a reasonable periodic-audit target in future runs.)*

### 4g Tone / Identity Drift — no findings

Scanned: recent CH-0008/CH-0009/CH-0010 affected CFs and their register commitments against WORLD_KERNEL.md tonal contract, chronotope, primary difference, §What Is Ordinary/Wonder/Taboo. CF-0034 AES-1 dry-veteran cadence, CF-0035 forbidden-trope list, CF-0036 aftermath-household-vocabulary cannibal-material template, and WORLD_KERNEL CH-0008/CH-0009/CH-0010 amendments to §What Is Ordinary / §What Is Wonder / §What Is Taboo all reinforce the kernel's lived-in-earthy-hazardous tone. Theme/Tone critic discipline is visibly load-bearing across CH-0008/CH-0009/CH-0010 adjudications. No drift detected.

### 4h Mystery Corruption — no findings

Scanned: MYSTERY_RESERVE.md entries M-1 through M-18 against recent CF `statement` and `notes` fields and against all OPEN_QUESTIONS items. Every MR entry carries a current disallowed-cheap-answer list, and CH-0008 / CH-0009 / CH-0010 firewall extensions add cross-application discipline (M-5 fifth cross-application per CH-0010 preserves the forbidden-resolution status). M-3 CH-0005 cross-reference explicitly separates civic-registry-scope coincidence from mechanistic parallel. No overexposure, no mystery-to-open-question silent drift.

### 4i Diegetic Leakage — 1 finding (F-03, below severity floor)

Scanned: `characters/vespera-nightwhisper.md` and `characters/melissa-threadscar.md`; `diegetic-artifacts/a-season-on-the-circuit.md` (DA-0001) and `diegetic-artifacts/after-action-report-harrowgate-contract.md` (DA-0002). Both artifacts carry comprehensive Canon Safety Check Traces covering all 18 MR entries plus all 16 invariants. DA-0002 Melissa's ARR explicitly declines M-16 (supremacist-cannibal doctrine origin) and scope-limits M-17 (prevalence) to "this corridor / this stretch / this thaw-season" — exactly the in-register firewall CH-0010 established. Vespera's dossier has the stale-MR-firewall-list issue (F-03, sev-2).

### 4j Local/Global Drift — 1 finding (F-02 → RP-0001)

Scanned: CFs with `scope.geographic: local` or `scope.social: restricted_group` against domain-file prose. CF-0031 / CF-0032 axis-count prose drift caught — see F-02.

## Findings

### F-01 — CH-0010 Change Log Entry Schema Drift (Severity 3)

**Category**: Phase 2 Change Log Delta + Rule 6 (No Silent Retcons) adjacent

**Cited anchors**: `CANON_LEDGER.md` lines 5432–5523 (CH-0010 full record)

**Description**: CH-0010 departs from the Change Log Entry schema stabilized across CH-0001 through CH-0009. Specifically:

- Uses `affected_cf_ids` AND `affected_fact_ids` (duplicate/redundant fields; prior CHs use only `affected_fact_ids`)
- Uses `affected_files` instead of `downstream_updates`
- `retcon_policy_checks` contains 4 different field names (`all_affected_files_updated`, `justification_stated`, `domain_consequences_propagated`, `mystery_reserve_impact_declared`) versus the prior stable 5-field set (`no_silent_edit`, `replacement_noted`, `no_stealth_diegetic_rewrite`, `net_contradictions_not_increased`, `world_identity_preserved`). FOUNDATIONS Rule 6 implicitly contracts on the latter form via CH-0001's genesis use.
- Missing `impact_on_existing_texts`, `severity_before_fix`, `severity_after_fix`, `reason` fields
- `scope` nested as `{geographic, temporal, social}` instead of flat `{local_or_global, changes_ordinary_life, creates_new_story_engines, mystery_reserve_effect}` (though CH-0010 does carry the three `changes_ordinary_life` / `creates_new_story_engines` / `mystery_reserve_effect` flags at top level, satisfying FOUNDATIONS §Change Control Policy semantically)
- Adds `initiating_proposal` field (absent in prior CHs)

**Severity rationale (3 — Structural Tension)**: The drift damages **mechanical auditability** — automated Rule 6 policy-check comparison across the 10 CHs cannot resolve against a single schema. FOUNDATIONS §Change Control Policy is satisfied semantically (record exists, affected files listed, local/global stated, ordinary-life/story-engines/mystery-reserve flagged). World identity is not at risk because the semantic content is present; but a future audit cycle or automated Rule 6 check would need per-CH schema resolution. Not Severity 4 because no world-truth contradiction is created.

**Escalation — requires user design decision**: This finding corresponds to NO `target_cf_id`. The drift is in the ledger's change-log metadata, not in any canon fact. Per skill §Phase 8, retcon cards require CF targets. **No RP-NNNN id consumed.** Options the user may pursue:

1. **Emit a `CH-0011` ledger-schema-harmonization entry** that restates CH-0010's metadata in the standard 9-field `retcon_policy_checks` set, with explicit mapping notes (preserves Rule 6 — no silent edit; additive annotation). This is the recommended option; it parallels the F-02 / RP-0001 clarification pattern.
2. Treat CH-0010 as an accepted schema variant; update future canon-addition workflow to harmonize, but leave the historical record as-is.
3. Manually patch `CANON_LEDGER.md` CH-0010 in-place. **Not recommended** — violates append-only norm and Rule 6.

Note for future canon-addition runs: the RP-0001 retcon card explicitly instructs CH-0011 to use the **standard** 9-field schema so that F-01's schema drift is not propagated into the clarification entry.

### F-02 — Polity-Asymmetric Axis Count Drift in Domain Files (Severity 3 → RP-0001)

**Category**: 4j (Local/Global Drift — prose-level scope miscount) + Rule 6 (No Silent Retcons) — acknowledged-but-unpatched inherited drift

**Cited anchors**:

- `TIMELINE.md` line 137 (CF-0031 Layer-4 bullet): *"a third axis of Charter-Era regional-variation friction alongside CF-0021 crafter-tolerance and CF-0024 mercenary-licensure. Three parallel polity-asymmetries now constitute a recognizable Charter-Era stress register"* — omits CF-0028/CF-0030 funder-primacy (added CH-0004 as the actual third axis)
- `INSTITUTIONS.md` line 40 (CF-0031 Law/Custom bullet): *"migration-gap extradition dispute class — a third axis of Charter-Era regional-variation friction alongside CF-0021 crafter-tolerance and CF-0024 mercenary-licensure"* — same undercount
- `INSTITUTIONS.md` line 169 (CF-0032 Trade/Guilds bullet): *"mirror image of the three disagreement-asymmetries CF-0021 / CF-0024 / CF-0028 / CF-0031"* — **lists four items but calls them "three"** (exact pathology PA-0006 flagged)
- `CANON_LEDGER.md` lines 538, 2658, 2737 — CF-record prose contains "three parallel asymmetries" language locked into CF-0031 and CF-0032 records

**Explicit prior acknowledgement**: `PA-0006-accept_with_required_updates.md` (CH-0007 adjudication) line 157: *"Pre-existing ledger inconsistency exposed: ledger slides between counting 'three axes' and naming four"* and line 296: *"pre-existing ledger counting-drift ('three axes' prose while naming four) noted as inherited; not created by this CF. No correction attempted in this adjudication."* CH-0007's CF-0033 record correctly counts to five, and `TIMELINE.md` line 138 explicitly acknowledges: *"the register formerly counted as three or four now stands at five"* — encoding the inconsistency as live canon. The drift is **known**, **acknowledged**, and **unpatched** across three CH cycles (CH-0007 → CH-0010).

**Severity rationale (3 — Structural Tension)**: The Charter-Era "polity-asymmetric stress register" is a load-bearing world pressure, and a reader of TIMELINE line 137 or INSTITUTIONS line 169 is misled about the count that held at CF-0031 / CF-0032 time. Not Severity 4 (World-Identity Risk) because CH-0007 onward correctly count, so present-state world identity is intact — the drift is localized to CH-0005/CH-0006-era prose annotations. Not Severity 2 (Soft Contradiction) because the inconsistency is structural (TIMELINE line 137 "three" vs TIMELINE line 138 "formerly counted as three or four, now five" — same file, internal disagreement).

**Inherited-drift handling**: The retcon card RP-0001 explicitly names this drift as inherited-from-CH-0005/CH-0006 and prescribes a Type-A Clarificatory repair that does not silently rewrite — it emits a corrected count with a cited `<!-- clarified by CH-0011 -->` annotation so the provenance trail is preserved. See `./AU-0001/retcon-proposals/RP-0001-polity-axis-count-clarification.md`.

### F-03 — Vespera Dossier Mystery-Reserve Firewall List Incomplete Against Current State (Severity 2, below floor)

**Category**: 4i (Diegetic Leakage — formal firewall list scope)

**Cited anchors**: `characters/vespera-nightwhisper.md` frontmatter `mystery_reserve_firewall` field (M-1 through M-7 only); `epistemic_horizon.cannot_know` list (M-1 through M-10, adds M-8–M-10)

**Description**: Vespera's dossier `generated_date: 2026-04-18` places her between CH-0003 (M-7 added) and CH-0004 (M-8/M-9/M-10 added). Her `mystery_reserve_firewall` frontmatter lists only M-1 through M-7, reflecting state-at-creation. Current world state (post-CH-0010) has M-1 through M-18. The dossier's actual knowledge claims do not leak against M-8 through M-18 content — she does not claim ruin-interior knowledge, beast-trophy knowledge, supremacist-doctrine knowledge — so **no forbidden-answer leakage is present**. The formal firewall list is stale against current state.

**Severity rationale (2 — Soft Contradiction)**: Lore underexplained rather than contradicted. The dossier was canon-safe at creation and remains canon-safe in practice; the gap is audit-trail completeness. Contrast with DA-0002 Melissa's ARR (2026-04-19) which correctly lists all 18 MRs — the pattern is "generated-date scopes firewall list." No retcon card because below severity floor of 3.

**Recommendation**: Optional low-effort refresh to amend Vespera's `mystery_reserve_firewall` list with M-8 through M-18 at "no-overlap" status. Non-critical — the pattern holds until a story leans on Vespera against newer MR surfaces. Character-generation skill may benefit from a periodic dossier-refresh sub-pass against current MR state, but that is a skill-design question, not a world-canon question.

---

## Burden Debt Trace (Phase 6)

Scanned all CFs with `type` in {capability, artifact, technology, institution, ritual, magic_practice} for post-acceptance consequence-free drift. Capabilities examined:

| CF | Type | Acceptance-time stabilizers | Subsequent CH interactions | Drift status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CF-0021 | capability (modern crafting) | unstable, rare parts, high failure, guild-monopolized containment clay, kiln-explosion risk, mobile crafter capital penalty | CH-0002 adds CF-0022 (vessel-hosted agency firewall) and CF-0023 (grimoire source) as STABILIZER reinforcements; CH-0003/CH-0004/CH-0005/CH-0007 touch the crafter-stream without loosening constraints | None — stabilizers reinforced |
| CF-0028 | institution (patron-funded expeditions) | guardian-construction squad-wipe (CF-0029); funder-primacy polity-asymmetric (CF-0030); anti-accumulation clause (CF-0027); M-10 presence heterogeneity firewall | CH-0008 extends anti-accumulation via shelter-scope firewall; CH-0009 distinct-origin firewall with CF-0029 | None — stabilizers reinforced |
| CF-0033 | institution (dragoons) | mission-scope negative clauses, transition-cost spike acknowledgment, four adoption-gates, anti-monopoly political cost | CH-0008 mission-scope negative-clause extension (no bandit-pursuit qua banditry); CH-0010 reform-current bifurcation third instance | None — stabilizers reinforced |
| CF-0034 | historical_process (endemic banditry) | worst-band self-suppression, corridor-local scope, raid-and-enslave sub-subset bound, M-10 selection-bias firewall | CH-0009 (CF-0035) trophy-legality distinction; CH-0010 (CF-0036) cannibal-edge sub-subset explicitly DISTINCT (not a new CF subtype) | None — stabilizers reinforced |
| CF-0035 | hazard (artifact-mutated beasts) | scale-cap (M-2 strict), non-sentience (M-5 fourth cross-application), non-guardian distinct-origin (M-8 bidirectional), forbidden-trope list, Rule-3 sub-specialty-not-cohort | CH-0010 (CF-0036) no cross-application to supremacist doctrine (pathological category error framing preserves M-5 fifth cross-application) | None — stabilizers reinforced |
| CF-0036 | historical_process (endemic interspecies friction) | SOC-1 world-level-preservation firewall, civic-suppression stabilizer, silenced-contractor absorbed into CF-0024 feeder (Rule 3), sealed-docket cannibal classification, supremacist doctrine as contested-canon never world-truth | (newest CF — no subsequent CHs yet) | No drift yet — stabilizer set is dense; monitor at next periodic audit |

**Finding**: **No burden debt detected.** Every capability CF accumulates stabilizers through subsequent CHs rather than eroding them. The pattern is notable — recent additions (CH-0008/CH-0009/CH-0010) visibly carry forward and extend prior stabilizer commitments, especially M-5 firewall (now fifth cross-application), M-10 firewall (now third cross-application), and Rule-3 absorption-not-new-subtype discipline. Continuity Archivist critic discipline in the canon-addition skill appears to be functioning at load-bearing level.

## Categories Deferred

None. All 10 Phase-4 sub-categories (4a–4j) audited. The large-file sampling disclosure is:

- `EVERYDAY_LIFE.md` (116KB) — section-header grep + targeted reads at cluster boundaries and CF-annotation presence checks. A deeper per-cluster text-audit is a reasonable target for a future periodic audit (AU-0002+).
- `INSTITUTIONS.md` (98KB) — section-header grep + targeted reads on the ten institutional domains, Law/Custom bullet enumeration (lines 35–45), Trade/Guilds bullets (lines 165–172), Military dragoon subtype line range.
- `ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md` (66KB) — section-header grep + full read of Breakage Points section (lines 137–160).
- `OPEN_QUESTIONS.md` (62KB) — read first 200 lines covering the deferral patterns for Place-and-Polity Naming through Reciprocal-Travel Case Law; later entries sampled via section-header grep.
- `MYSTERY_RESERVE.md` (131KB) — read M-1 through M-6 in detail; M-7 onward sampled via grep for "disallowed cheap answers" discipline and cross-application firewall extensions.

None of the deferred-depth surfaces surfaced severity-3+ findings at section-header-plus-targeted-read depth. Future audits should budget deeper reads of EVERYDAY_LIFE and MYSTERY_RESERVE as routine practice.
